inputs.fcf_source: FY2025 cash from operations $3.26B less estimated maintenance capital spending $1.35B, divided by 222.7M diluted shares (10-K FY2025); GAAP FCF negative due to NEOM/JIGPC growth capex
inputs.normalization_note: Core industrial gases earn adjusted operating income $2.9B and adjusted diluted EPS $12.03; Lawrence base uses maintenance-level owner cash, not growth project spend or FY2025 $3.7B project-exit charges

bear.notes: Further clean-energy write-downs; merchant volumes soft; multiple compresses toward industrial peers

base.notes: Core on-site/merchant oligopoly persists; project exits reduce growth capex drag; NEOM option not in base cash path

bull.notes: NEOM hydrogen-ammonia ramps; pricing holds; capital discipline after Louisiana/Casa Grande exits

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: NEOM Green Hydrogen VIE; JIGPC 55% equity affiliate — fair value not fully marked in Lawrence base (10-K FY2025 Note 3, Note 26)

option_scan.question: Undeveloped reserves / dormant assets?
option_scan.evidence: Multi-billion-dollar NEOM facility under construction; 8-K 2026-06-30 project update — not in base owner cash

option_scan.question: In-business loss segment?
option_scan.treatment_rationale: Louisiana, Casa Grande, World Energy exits; corporate operating loss $367M FY2025 — burden in GAAP, excluded from normalized base
option_scan.evidence: 8-K 2026-06-26 up to $2.9B pre-tax charge; FY2025 business and asset actions $3.7B (10-K Note 5)

option_scan.question: Backlog / contracted revenue not in FCF path?
option_scan.evidence: On-site take-or-pay and merchant contracts; financing revenue $1.14B FY2025 in Americas segment — modeled via core growth, not separate terminal

option_scan.question: Private or illiquid stakes below fair value?
option_scan.evidence: 55% JIGPC interest; $1.6B initial plus $908M follow-on investments (10-K segment note)

option_scan.question: Material options after scan?
option_scan.answer: NEOM hydrogen option; core gases sufficient for Lawrence gate
option_scan.evidence: Option overlay separate from conservative Lawrence base; base uses maintenance owner cash only

growth_explanation.mechanism: Core industrial gases: on-site take-or-pay volumes plus merchant pricing (2% non-helium FY2025) and energy cost pass-through; growth capped by project-exit cycle and capital allocation reset
growth_explanation.source: 10-K FY2025 MD&A; 10-Q Q2 FY2026 adjusted EPS +19% YoY

falsifiers.observation: Merchant pricing turns negative two consecutive fiscal years outside helium
Additional multi-billion project charges beyond June 2026 Louisiana/Casa Grande exit
falsifiers.action: Downgrade moat to eroding; cap exit multiple at 15x

paths.label: Growth theory (pre-falsifier)
paths.weight_why: Segment blend before falsifier pass; diagnostic only when it differs from falsifier-adjusted.
weight_falsifiers: Included at full weight alongside falsifier-adjusted without divergence note

paths.label: Bear scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Downside stress; low weight because bear is a sensitivity, not a competing explanation of base mechanics.
weight_falsifiers: Bear assumptions become base case in filings (e.g. sustained volume collapse)
paths.hard_to_vary: yes: asymmetric low weight is structural

paths.label: Bull scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Upside stress probe; higher than bear because optionality archetype has skew, but still below primary paths.
weight_falsifiers: Bull growth sustained in filings for 4+ quarters without falsifier trigger

qualitative_adjustments.factor: No dated predictive attribute at spot
qualitative_adjustments.rationale: Scarcity is descriptive; no timed mispricing catalyst in base
qualitative_adjustments.falsifier: Cross-check names dated catalyst with timeline

deutsch_checks.reach: Weights encode evidence tier (A primary → D external), not fitted to hit a target IRR

synthesis.notes: Capstone IRR combining filings, segments, overlays, third-party, qualitative
